---
title: "LRP61: Mechanism model — letter-sound (LS) -> word reading (WR), moderated by phoneme blending (PA)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP61 is a **joint-readiness** interaction model. It extends [LRP58](../lrp-rli-mech-058) —
the mechanism estimate $f^L$ of letter-sound knowledge on word reading — with a **linear
moderation** by **phoneme blending** $B$ (`blending`, PA), the first *code-route* moderator
in the joint-readiness family (the companions [LRP71](../lrp-rli-mech-071)/93/94/95 moderate
the same $L \to W$ curve by vocabulary). Its nearest sibling [LRP72](../lrp-rli-mech-072)
asks the same $L \times B$ question on *decoding* (nonword) and came out sub-additive
($\gamma_{\text{int}} = -0.33$, 89% CrI $-0.57$ to $-0.09$); LRP61 moves the outcome to word
reading, the cell the mechanism suite otherwise leaves open.

The mediation suite already shows the intervention's word-reading gain does not measurably
run *through* blending (`med-066` $\mathrm{NIE}_B \approx -0.03$ words, P=42%; `med-075`
$L \to B \to W \approx 0$, P=42%). LRP61 asks the different question of whether the
letter-sound $\to$ word-reading **conversion is gated by** blending — whether letter sounds
only translate into word reading once blending is in place.

Letter sounds enter as the nonparametric **HSGP** curve $f^L$ (as in LRP58); phoneme blending
enters as a standardised linear main effect plus the interaction:

$$
\eta = \dots + f^{L}(\operatorname{logit} L_{\text{post}})
       + \gamma_{\text{mod}}\, z(\operatorname{logit} B_{\text{post}})
       + \gamma_{\text{int}}\, z(\operatorname{logit} L_{\text{post}})\, z(\operatorname{logit} B_{\text{post}})
$$

where $z(\cdot)$ standardises to mean 0, sd 1 over the fitted rows. The moderator enters on the
**same logit-safe scale as the exposure**: the bounded blending proportion is Haldane-logit
transformed and then standardised, so $\gamma_{\text{int}}$ reads per +1 SD of the
*logit-transformed* blending proportion, not per raw-score SD. Both cross-coupling coefficients
use the regularising $\mathrm{Normal}(0, 0.3)$ prior.

_Reading the interaction (logit scale)._ $\gamma_{\text{int}}$ is the change, **on the latent
logit linear predictor**, in the letter-sound -> word-reading slope per +1 SD of logit blending.
The labels below describe this logit-scale product term, *not* additivity in predicted word
*counts*: $\gamma_{\text{int}} \approx 0$ means no product term on the logit, which the
Beta-Binomial link still maps to a mild interaction in item counts, and the sign alone does not
establish that word reading is highest when both skills are high (an item-scale statement — read
the posterior curve for that).

- $\gamma_{\text{int}} > 0$ = **synergy** (logit scale): the letter-sound slope is steeper where
  blending is also high.
- $\gamma_{\text{int}} \approx 0$ = **additive** (logit scale): no logit-scale product term.
- $\gamma_{\text{int}} < 0$ = **substitutive** (logit scale): a shallower joint slope.

## Adjustment set

The revised LRP58 $L \to W$ set (#245): $\{G, A, HS, IS(\text{attend}), SP, W_{\text{pre}}\}$ —
group, linear age, hearing, intervention sessions, speech production, and baseline word reading.
Phoneme blending $B$ enters **additionally**, via its main effect and interaction only.

_Blending is a downstream skill, not a parallel prerequisite._ Unlike the vocabulary moderators,
blending $B = \mathrm{PA}$ is a **DAG-descendant of the exposure** $L$ ($\mathrm{LS} \to \mathrm{PA}$).
Conditioning on it therefore makes $f^L$ a **controlled-direct** effect of letter sounds *at fixed
blending*, and $\gamma_{\text{int}}$ is **effect-modification by a downstream skill**, not a
symmetric "need both" prerequisite test — the same estimand caveat documented on
[LRP72](../lrp-rli-mech-072). Blending has a 10-item ceiling.

_Adjusted association, not causal._ Latent general ability ($GA$) is not adjusted for, so
everything here — $f^L$, $\gamma_{\text{mod}}$ and $\gamma_{\text{int}}$ — is a
**latent-GA-confounded adjusted association**, never a causal effect. The randomised causal claim
lives in the ITT suite.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
